title: fritillaria lusitanica subsp. neglecta
intro: Fritillaria lusitanica subsp. neglecta, also known as the Iberian fritillary, is a species of flowering plant in the lily family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating coughs, and is often used to treat bronchitis and other respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs and rhizomes are commonly used to create teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
